lesson.menu_desc: Finalizing a deal & reported speech (He said the price was final) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Closing the Deal (Finalizing and Reported Speech)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael Gasparelli Lima | Aula 19 -- Closing the Deal

listenings.text: Good afternoon. I am calling to confirm that we are ready to finalize our agreement. Both parties have reviewed the final version, and we are happy with the terms. The effective date will be the first of next month. We will need two witnesses for the signing, and each side will keep one signed copy. If you agree, we will prepare the documents this week. Once everything is signed, the agreement becomes binding, and both companies must follow it. We are very pleased to close this deal with you. Please confirm in writing, and we will move forward. Thank you for your trust, and welcome to a long partnership.

Let me tell you what happened in our last big negotiation. At the first meeting, the client said the price was too high. Our director told them that the quality was worth it. Then the client said they needed more time to decide. We said we understood, but we explained that the offer would expire at the end of the month. A week later, the client called and told us they accepted the terms. They said they would sign on Monday. On Monday, both sides met, the lawyers checked every clause, and we signed the final agreement. The client told me later that it was the smoothest deal of the year. Patience and clear words closed that deal.

extra_audio.text: First, both parties review the final version of the agreement. Then, they confirm in writing that they accept all the terms. Next, they set the effective date and invite the witnesses. After that, each side signs the document and keeps one copy. Finally, the agreement becomes binding, and both companies must follow it.
